what_it_does: Implements a module-level React subscription store for a transcript-search surface: it holds the controlled query string, the full /api/transcript-search response envelope, loading/error flags, and an open/closed flag, and exposes setters, a search thunk, and a useTranscript hook.
observations: Uses a module-level mutable singleton (state + Set<()=>void>) rather than Context or Redux.
Stores the entire search response envelope so a downstream adapter can read q, mode_used, and semantic.available.
Search is query-driven: opening only sets open=true; no fetch happens until a non-empty query is submitted.
Guards against stale responses with a monotonically increasing loadSeq.
Empty query is a no-op; errors produce a single user-facing string.
The subscribe hook forces re-renders with useState increment pattern.
Comments include a live-verified data-shape contract from a running bridge.
standouts: Full-response passthrough design: the store keeps the whole API envelope, not just result rows, so the renderer can see mode_used and semantic availability.
Honest-degrade semantics are explicitly documented: semantic scores are real when the embedder is up, lexical mode is marked degraded.
Stale-request cancellation via loadSeq rather than AbortController.
conventions: Follows the 'ONE subscribe-store pattern' mirrored from channelStore/decisionsStore.
Controlled input state lives in global store to survive re-renders.
TypeScript types are declared before module state and functions.
Fetch URL is built with encodeURIComponent.
Module exports named action functions and a useTranscript hook.
concerns: Module-level mutable state can leak across tests and concurrent consumers.
Pending fetches are not aborted on unmount or newer query; only the response is ignored.
Error details are swallowed in the catch block, losing HTTP status and stack.
No debounce/throttle: every non-empty query triggers a network request immediately.
k=24 and the endpoint are hardcoded with no configuration surface.
open/close is only a boolean with no URL, history, or focus management.
notes: A minimal, query-driven subscription store that keeps the whole transcript-search envelope for a constellation renderer, with explicit loading and honest error states.
questions: Which component calls openTranscript/closeTranscript and renders the constellation?
How is the adapter (DNA.faceRecord.transcriptRecord) wired to consume this store?
Is there a debounce or throttle between keystrokes and searchTranscript calls?
How are sessions identified beyond the session_address string?
What UI surfaces the error string to the user?
Is there SSR/hydration handling for the module-level state?
What fields does index contain and are they used by the renderer?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual: it is a query-driven store that holds the full search response and exposes search/open/close/query controls.
intention_signals: It appears designed to be a small, self-contained data layer for a semantic transcript-search UI that renders results as a star constellation, while preserving metadata about search mode and embedder availability so the renderer can communicate result quality to the user.
novelty: Storing the entire API response envelope (including mode_used and semantic availability) rather than normalizing to rows, so that downstream DNA rendering can encode honest quality/degrade signals in the visualization.
contribution: Contributes a query-driven subscription-store pattern with full-response passthrough, explicit loading/error states, and a stale-request guard; the idea of carrying the whole search envelope (query, mode used, semantic availability) to the renderer so that quality signals can be visualized rather than hidden.
summary_for_embedding: A React subscription store module that manages the controlled query, loading state, and full /api/transcript-search response envelope for a transcript corpus constellation visualization.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to back a search-by-meaning transcript surface where the whole search response—including mode and semantic availability—is passed through to a DNA renderer for honest quality signaling.

symbols.does: Declares the shape of one transcript search result row.
symbols.description: A TypeScript type describing a matched session node, including session_address, optional title/name, hit count, score, state, and a text snippet. This is the per-session result shape used inside the results array.

symbols.does: Declares the full search API response envelope.
symbols.description: A TypeScript type extending Record<string, unknown> to capture the entire /api/transcript-search response, including ok, q, mode_used, semantic availability, chunk/session counts, and the results array. The store deliberately keeps this whole envelope so the adapter can read metadata beyond rows.

symbols.does: Declares the internal store state shape.
symbols.description: A TypeScript type for the module-level state object, tracking the controlled query string, the last full search response, loading and error flags, and whether the surface is open.

symbols.does: Applies a partial state patch and notifies all subscribers.
symbols.description: A private helper that merges a partial update into the module-level state object and iterates the subscription Set to force re-renders in every subscribed React component.

symbols.does: Updates the controlled query text in store state.
symbols.description: A public action that writes the current input text into state so a controlled <input> can survive re-renders without local-state races; it does not trigger a search.

symbols.does: Fetches /api/transcript-search for a trimmed query and stores the full response.
symbols.description: An async public action that increments a sequence number, sets loading, calls the transcript search endpoint with k=24, validates ok, and writes the full response envelope into state. It ignores stale responses and sets a generic error message on failure.

symbols.does: Marks the transcript surface as open.
symbols.description: A public action that sets open=true. The surface is query-driven, so opening does not itself load any data; it only reveals the search prompt.

symbols.does: Marks the transcript surface as closed.
symbols.description: A public action that sets open=false, hiding the search surface without clearing query or results.

symbols.does: Subscribes a React component to the module-level store state.
symbols.description: A React hook that registers a force-update callback in the subscription Set during an effect and returns the current state object, enabling components to re-render when any store field changes.
A local closure inside useTranscript that increments a dummy useState counter, causing the host component to re-render whenever the store notifies subscribers.
